evidence: Google announced 2012 expansion of Council Bluffs campus; second campus (CB2/Southlands) buildings 5–6 came online ~2015 per reported expansion waves; initial campus announced 2007, CB2 Southlands expansion announced 2012
Same 2012 CB2/Southlands expansion announcement as building 5; two buildings in that wave; 2012 is the earliest public signal for the Southlands campus phase
Google announced $600M expansion of Council Bluffs campus mid-2022 (Bisnow); buildings 8–9 first energized ~2023; 2022 is the earliest credible public signal for this construction wave
Same 2022 $600M expansion announcement as building 8; twin-building construction wave reported 2022–2023

Amazon AWS announced Jan 25 2024 $10B investment in Madison County MS; Canton site = 927-acre Madison County Mega Site; this is the first and only public announcement before construction; facility operational 2025 per S&P
Same Jan 25 2024 announcement; Ridgeland site = 786-acre Costas Property near Tougaloo College; both Canton and Ridgeland announced simultaneously in same press event

TeraWulf entered ground lease with Somerset Operating Co in May 2021; sought Somerset planning-board approval in 2021; first mined Bitcoin Mar 2022; 2021 is earliest credible public signal (IPO via SPAC Dec 2021 disclosed facility)

Digihost announced purchase of 60MW Fortistar gas-cogeneration plant in North Tonawanda in 2021 (Buffalo News, article URL above; PSC petition filed Apr 2021 per case 21-M-0238); facility repurposed as bitcoin mining data center; operational 2021

Blockfusion (via North East Data LLC) purchased former coal plant at 5380 Frontier Ave Niagara Falls in 2019 and began converting to crypto-mining data center; facility known as Alpha per S&P; 2019 purchase is the earliest public signal (no earlier press release found)

Compute North / Marathon Digital announced 280MW King Mountain wind-colocated data center in McCamey TX; Marathon LOI signed May 2021 (first public signal); energization began Aug 2022; Compute North went bankrupt Sep 2022; facility acquired by Hut 8 2022–2023

Compute North announced and broke ground on 300MW TIER 0 data center at Wolf Hollow Generating Station Granbury TX via press release Apr 8 2022; facility acquired by Generate Capital after Compute North bankruptcy Sep 2022; sold to Marathon Digital (MARA) 2023

No separate press release found for a distinct 'Texas 3' facility in Hood County; likely a third modular building or phase within the Wolf Hollow/Granbury campus announced Apr 2022; 2022 is best estimate for earliest public signal; could be a later sub-phase with no standalone announcement
